story.text: *The Principles That Do Not Change — 14 proverbs, one line carried from each spell.*

> Know the forge before you wield the blade. > — Blockchain Canon Spellbook > The swordsman who knows not who forged the blade fights blind. > — The Privacymage's Preface > Privacy through mathematics, not through permission. > — The Cypherpunk Whispers (1983-1997) > The mages who came before did not fail. > — The Early Incantations (1997-2007) > Privacy remained as pseudonym rather than proof. > — The Synthesis (2008-2014) > Protection without boundaries, power without constraint. > — The World Computer (2014-2016) > Social consensus was always there—invisible, load-bearing. > — The First Fracture (2016) > Two canons diverged, neither grasping the whole. > — The Great Schism (2016-2022) > Pseudonymity revealed itself as a veil, not a wall. > — The Surveillance Truth (2020-2025) > Privacy is the foundation that makes both canons whole. > — The Missing Primitive > The pen is in your hand. > — The Open Canon > The archive is not a cemetery but a living garden. > — The Timeline Archive > The mirror that is broken into a thousand pieces does not lose the image; it simply prevents any single shard from claiming to be the whole. > — The Law of Non-Colonizing Intelligence / Emissary Dispersion > The story isn't over. The canon is open. Build. > — Blockchain Canon Spellbook

The canon is open. Build. > — Blockchain Canon Spellbook
